filling: Greetings, sir. I hope I did not startle you - I was resting when I happened to glimpse you here. Forgive my impertinence, but might I ask your name and profession? I am Chen Zian, a scholar from the provinces, preparing

Greetings, young scholar. No need for apologies - I was merely resting under the trees. I am An Lushan, a merchant of foreign goods - spices, glassware, and textiles from the Silk Road. I see you are a man of learning. T

A merchant of foreign goods from the Silk Road! How fascinating. I must confess, as a scholar preparing for the imperial examination, I am most curious about matters of the court. Do your travels bring you into contact w

Indeed, young scholar, my trade does bring me to the attention of various officials and occasionally the court itself. The foreign glassware and spices are prized by many a gentleman of means. But tell me more of your st

Thank you for your curiosity, sir. I study the Confucian classics and practice regulated verse, hoping to distinguish myself in the imperial examination. Since you mention contact with officials and the court, I wonder -

The court indeed holds many opportunities for a merchant such as myself. But enough of my humble trade - you speak of the imperial examination, the path to officialdom. A scholar of your talents surely has bright prospec

You are too kind, sir. I am still a long way from such prospects, and much study remains. Since your trade brings you into contact with officials and the court, I am most curious - do you happen to hear any rumors or kno

Rumors? Well, young scholar, the court is always bustling with whispers - who rises, who falls, which policies favor which goods. But such talk is delicate, you understand. Perhaps we could discuss this further over tea?

That is most generous of you, sir. I would be honored to share tea with you and hear more of what you have observed. As a provincial scholar unfamiliar with the capital's ways, any insight you might share about the court

Indeed, I would be honored to share what I know over tea. A scholar like yourself must have many connections among the educated families of the city. Perhaps you could recommend a good teahouse? And in return, I could te

Tea would be most welcome, sir. I know a pleasant tea house not far from here - the establishment run by Li Mingzhu, if you are familiar with it. There we could speak more freely. I am particularly interested in any insi

But tell me, young scholar - as you study for the examinations, you must know many families of means, yes? Perhaps even some who appreciate foreign wares? A merchant like myself always seeks gentlemen who recognize quali

Tea would be most welcome, sir. I would be honored to hear more of what you have observed about court matters. As I prepare for the examinations, any insight into the preferences of those who judge such papers would be i

There is a fine tea house not far from here, run by a lady named Li Mingzhu. The tea is good and the atmosphere pleasant for conversation. If you have time, we could continue our talk there. A scholar such as yourself mi

That is most generous of you, sir. I would be honored to join you for tea. There is so much I wish to learn - not merely rumors, but also matters pertaining to the imperial examination itself. I have wondered whether the

My treat, of course. There is a fine tea house not far from here - the White Jade Tea House, run by a woman called Li Mingzhu. The tea is excellent and the setting private enough for good conversation. I would be honored

node_12.subject: self
node_12.description: He feels calmer when he copies a difficult passage until the brushwork settles his thoughts.
node_12.embedding_key: He feels calmer when he copies a difficult passage until the brushwork settles his thoughts.

node_11.subject: self
node_11.description: He promised himself he would not return home without giving the examinations his best effort.
node_11.embedding_key: He promised himself he would not return home without giving the examinations his best effort.

node_10.subject: self
node_10.description: Festival distractions make concentration harder, but he also knows social encounters can yield useful advice.
node_10.embedding_key: Festival distractions make concentration harder, but he also knows social encounters can yield useful advice.

node_9.subject: self
node_9.description: He is curious whether foreign merchants hear rumors of court priorities before local scholars do.
node_9.embedding_key: He is curious whether foreign merchants hear rumors of court priorities before local scholars do.

node_8.subject: self
node_8.description: He intends to ask anyone well-informed whether current examiners value moral clarity over ornament.
node_8.embedding_key: He intends to ask anyone well-informed whether current examiners value moral clarity over ornament.

node_7.subject: self
node_7.description: He envies candidates who seem naturally graceful with parallel prose.
node_7.embedding_key: He envies candidates who seem naturally graceful with parallel prose.

node_6.subject: self
node_6.description: He once impressed a county magistrate by composing a neat regulated verse on short notice.
node_6.embedding_key: He once impressed a county magistrate by composing a neat regulated verse on short notice.

node_5.subject: self
node_5.description: He respects Li Mingzhu because her tea house often gathers clerks, scholars, and travelers with useful news.
node_5.embedding_key: He respects Li Mingzhu because her tea house often gathers clerks, scholars, and travelers with useful news.

node_4.subject: self
node_4.description: He has heard that the preferences of court examiners shift with factional politics.
node_4.embedding_key: He has heard that the preferences of court examiners shift with factional politics.

node_3.subject: self
node_3.description: He worries that his prose is sound but not elegant enough for metropolitan examiners.
node_3.embedding_key: He worries that his prose is sound but not elegant enough for metropolitan examiners.

node_2.subject: self
node_2.description: He can still recite passages from the Analects that his village tutor drilled into him as a child.
node_2.embedding_key: He can still recite passages from the Analects that his village tutor drilled into him as a child.

node_1.subject: self
node_1.description: His father sold part of the family grain store to fund this journey to Chang'an.
node_1.embedding_key: His father sold part of the family grain store to fund this journey to Chang'an.
